notes:
tay chân = limbs (arms and legs). It usually refers to both arms and legs together, not just one.

It’s a noun and often used as a fixed pair, so you don’t normally count it separately. Common with words about movement, injury, tiredness, or temperature.

It also has a very common figurative meaning: someone’s subordinates or henchmen (people who do things for them). In this sense, it’s slightly negative.

Strong link: think of tay (hand/arm) + chân (leg) → the main parts that help you act or move.
exampleSentences: Sau khi tập thể dục, anh cảm thấy tay chân hơi mệt.
Bé bị ngã nên tay chân có vài vết xước nhỏ.
Trời lạnh quá, em nên giữ ấm tay chân khi ra ngoài.
exampleSentencesTranslated: After exercising, he feels his limbs are a bit tired.
The child fell down so his limbs have a few small scratches.
It's very cold, you should keep your limbs warm when going outside.
